sanskrit: मन्मना भव मद्भक्तो मद्याजी मां नमस्कुरु । मामेवैष्यसि युक्त्वैवमात्मानं मत्परायणः ॥
hindiTranslation: तू मेरा भक्त हो जा, मेरेमें मनवाला हो जा, मेरा पूजन करनेवाला हो जा और मेरेको नमस्कार कर ।  इस प्रकार मेरे साथ अपनेआपको लगाकर, मेरे परायण हुआ तू मेरेको ही प्राप्त होगा । , ॥
englishTranslation: Fix your mind on me. Be devoted to me. Worship me. Bow to me. United with me in this way, and fully oriented toward me, you will come to me.
essence: Total orientation toward the divine becomes the way to reach the divine.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: Krishna asks Arjuna to place his mind, devotion, worship, and respect entirely on him. He promises that this full orientation will lead Arjuna to him.
simpleInsight: Devotion is not passive feeling; it becomes a complete direction for the mind and life.
detailedMeaning: On the battlefield of Kurukshetra, Arjuna still stands frozen while Krishna gives the closing note of his teaching on divine presence. After showing that even the unlikely and excluded can reach the highest state, Krishna now gives the direct practice: orient everything toward him.
modernRelevance: You are standing outside a hospital room, deciding whether to tell the truth that will change everything. This verse asks for total alignment, not half-hearted loyalty: let the mind, intention, and action point in one direction.
translationLiteral: मत्-मनाः (mind on me) / भव (become) / मत्-भक्तः (my devotee) / मत्-याजी (my worshipper) / माम् (to me) / नमस्कुरु (bow) / माम् (me) / एव (only) / एष्यसि (you will come) / युक्त्वा (having united) / एवम् (thus) / आत्मानम् (yourself) / मत्-परायणः (with me as your highest goal)
insight: It shifts devotion from emotion alone to a full-life orientation that gathers thought, action, and surrender into one movement.

quoteVariants.text: Keep your mind on me. Bow, worship, and come home to me.
Mind on me. Heart toward me.
The mind that stays with me arrives at me.
Fix your mind on me today.
